title: secamone lankawiensis
intro: Secamone lankawiensis, also known as the Lankawi Secamone, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to Peninsular Malaysia and Thailand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Secamone lankawiensis include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, where the leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to release their medicinal properties.
